fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Alabama Respiratory Therapist License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a respiratory therapist? Here's whether your license transfers to Alabama - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: respiratory therapist license reciprocity alabama, how to transfer respiratory therapist license to alabama, respiratory therapist compact alabama, alabama respiratory therapist endorsement application

board.name: Alabama State Board of Respiratory Therapy
board.mailing_address:
Alabama State Board of Respiratory Therapy
P.O. Box 242767
Montgomery, AL 36124-2767

quick_facts.exam_name: National Board for Respiratory Care (NBRC) entry level examination

license_types.title: Registered Respiratory Therapist (RRT)
license_types.description: The standard credential to practice as a respiratory therapist in Alabama.

steps.title: Create an Account
steps.description: Access the ALBRT Online Application System and register for a new user account by selecting 'Register Now' and following the on-screen instructions to set up your login credentials.

steps.title: Start Application
steps.description: Log in to your ALBRT Online Application System account and select the appropriate application type, such as 'New Licensure Application' or 'Licensure by Endorsement Application', to begin.

steps.title: Complete Online Form
steps.description: Fill out all required sections of the online application within the ALBRT Online Application System, providing accurate personal, educational, and professional history.

steps.title: Submit Supporting Documents
steps.description: Upload all necessary supporting documents, which typically include official transcripts from your respiratory therapy program, verification of NBRC credentials (RRT or CRT), and verification of any active licenses held in other states, directly through the ALBRT Online Application System.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Remit the required application fee, such as the $150 endorsement fee, securely through the ALBRT Online Application System.

steps.title: Board Review and License Issuance
steps.description: The Alabama State Board of Respiratory Therapy will review your complete application and supporting documents. Upon approval, your Alabama Respiratory Therapist license will be issued.

documents.detail: Sent directly from your educational institution.
Official verification of passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam.

provisional_text: Many states offer a limited permit or provisional license for new graduates waiting to take the NBRC. Check specific board rules for supervisory requirements.
reciprocity_text: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

faqs.question: How much does a Alabama respiratory therapist license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial application and licensing fee varies, often around $100 to $200. Check the specific fee breakdown above.

faqs.question: Is national certification required?
faqs.answer: Yes, passing the NBRC (National Board for Respiratory Care) TMC Exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

faqs.question: Does Alabama have reciprocity for respiratory therapists?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers respiratory therapist practice in Alabama. To work in Alabama, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my respiratory therapist license to Alabama?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Alabama while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Respiratory Therapists licensed in another state can often apply via endorsement by providing license verification and current certification.

hero.title: Become a Respiratory Therapist in Alabama
hero.dek: To apply for a Respiratory Therapist license in Alabama, applicants must create an account and submit their application through the ALBRT Online Application System.

official_links.registration_steps: Navigate to the ALBRT Online Application System URL, click 'Register Now' for a new user account, follow the prompts to create an account with an email address and password, then verify your account to log in and access application forms.
